notes: Core general meeting service; continues as Cisco Webex Meetings under Cisco ownership post-2007
Third-generation private conferencing switched network infrastructure; proprietary multimedia switching; evolved into Cisco Webex infrastructure; brand name retired
Launched April 2002; specialized service for corporate training delivery with scheduling, registration, SCORM support; continues as Cisco Webex Training
Specialized service for live remote technical customer support; continues as Cisco Webex Support Center
Specialized service for large online events and webinars; continues as Cisco Webex Events (formerly Webex Webinars)
Category combining browser-based presentation sharing with audio; emerging in 2002; now dominant work infrastructure via Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Cisco Webex
Traditional dedicated-room video systems using ISDN; mature in 2002; largely supplanted by software-based solutions; niche hardware market remains
Traditional telephone bridge conferencing; mature in 2002; largely subsumed into web/video conferencing platforms
Internet voice protocol; cited as nascent component of web conferencing in 2002; now dominant telecommunications protocol globally
Integrated Services Digital Network; used for business-to-business video conferencing in 2002 due to IP security concerns; now obsolete; most PSTN ISDN services decommissioned by 2020s
Microsoft presentation software; central use case for web conferencing (sharing slides); remains active and widely used
Proprietary WebEx format enabling full PowerPoint animation support in web meetings including Flash, media players, and 3D CAD; proprietary format evolved under Cisco; not a public standard
Sharable Content Object Reference Model; e-learning data interchange standard; supported by WebEx Training Center; remains active in e-learning industry though supplemented by xAPI/Tin Can
Handheld computing devices (Palm, Pocket PC); supported by WebEx Mediatone for meeting participation; category obsolete by ~2010, replaced by smartphones
Multimedia platform used to embed rich media in PowerPoint presentations shared via WebEx UCF; Adobe discontinued Flash Player December 31 2020; all browser support ended
